steps.title: Express x in terms of y from the first inequality
Apply the y-constraint to find the bound on x
steps.content:
The second constraint states $y > 3$. Since $y$ must exceed 3, we can determine what happens to the upper limit of $x$:
  • If $y > 3$, then $2y > 6$
  • Therefore $-2y < -6$
  • So $2 - 2y < 2 - 6 = -4$

Since $x < 2 - 2y$ and $2 - 2y < -4$, we conclude that $x < -4$. That matches choice D — D is correct.
